TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-01-2013 IN 504, DEFENDANTS HEALTH NET OF CALIFORNIA, INC AND HEALTH NET, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED AS TO HEALTH NET, INC. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE PARENT COMPANY WAS INVOLVED. THE OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE FROM A THIRD PARTY WEBSITE IS SUSTAINED. OTHERWISE DENIED BECAUSE THERE REMAIN TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER THE SURGERY WAS MEDICALLY NECESSARY AND WHETHER DEFENDANT CONDUCTED AN ADEQUATE INVESTIGATION BEFORE DENYING PLAINTIFF'S APPEALS. GIVEN ALL THE CIRCUMSTANCES, IT WILL BE FOR THE TRIER OF FACT TO DECIDE WHETHER, IF IT FINDS ANY BAD FAITH CONDUCT, SUCH CONDUCT IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY AN AWARD UNDER THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM OR TO AWARD PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH C.C.P. 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHEILA ARCENO OF VERITEXT LEGAL SOLUTIONS, CSR #13293 (866.299.5127)
ORDER REGARDING CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT AND STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER

OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF SCOTT, BONNIE

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HILL PHYSICIANS MEDICAL GROUP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CLAIMS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN BECAUSE DEFENDANT CAN REASONABLY RESPOND TO THEM. PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY PLEADS A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR AND FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICES. PLAINTIFF PLEADS INJURY IN FACT AND MAY BE ABLE TO OBTAIN INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST THIS DEFENDANT. THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALSO ADEQUATELY PLED. PLAINTIFFS PLEADS THAT DEFENDANT'S DENIAL OF HER MEDICALLY NECESSARY TREATMENT HAS CAUSED HARM TO HER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

Defendant Hill Physicians Medical Group'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HILL PHYSICIANS MEDICAL GROUP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Appearances:
Travis Corby of Shernoff Bidard Echeverria Bentley (310.246.0503), Conor Granahan of the Law Offices of Conor Granahan (415.830.3325), and David M. Lilienstein of the DL Law Group (415.271.7169) for plaintiff and responding party;
Dennis G. Rolstad of Sedgwick LLP (415.781.7900) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted as to the 17200 cause of action because Plaintiff cannot show Defendant acted unfairly within the meaning of that statute. Otherwise denied, because the finder of fact will have to determine whether Defendant's denial was a proximate cause of Plaintiff not getting the surgery. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order that complies with C.C.P. 437 c(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sheila Arceno of Veritext Legal Solutions, CSR #13293 (866.299.5127)

Defendants Health Net Of California, Health Net, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS HEALTH NET OF CALIFORNIA, INC AND HEALTH NET, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Appearances:
Travis Corby of Shernoff Bidard Echeverria Bentley (310.246.0503), Conor Granahan of the Law Offices of Conor Granahan (415.830.3325), and David M. Lilienstein of the DL Law Group (415.271.7169) for plaintiff and responding party;
Gregory N. Pimstone and Jeffrey J. Maurer of Manatt Phelps & Phillips, LLP (310.312.4132) for defendants and moving parties.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted as to Health Net, Inc. because Plaintiff has no admissible evidence that the parent company was involved. The objection to Plaintiff's evidence from a third party website is sustained. Otherwise denied because there remain triable issues of fact whether the surgery was medically necessary and whether Defendant conducted an adequate investigation before denying Plaintiff's appeals. Given all the circumstances, it will be for the trier of fact to decide whether, if it finds any bad faith conduct, such conduct is sufficient to justify an award under the intentional infliction of emotional distress claim or to award punitive damages. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order that complies with C.C.P. 437 c(g). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sheila Arceno of Veritext Legal Solutions, CSR #13293 (866.299.5127)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID M. LILIENSTEIN & ATTY. CONOR GRANAHAN OF DL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 271-7169 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL SCANLON JR. OF SEDGWICK LLP, TEL. NO. 781-7900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HILL PHYSICIANS MEDICAL GROUP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The fourth and fifth claims are not uncertain because defendant can reasonably respond to them. Plaintiff adequately pleads a cause of action for unfair and fraudulent business practices. Plaintiff pleads injury in fact and may be able to obtain injunctive relief against this defendant. The negligence cause of action is also adequately pled. Plaintiffs pleads that defendant's denial of her medically necessary treatment has caused harm to her. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
